seo.title: Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents in DSPy Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix tool loop detection in ai agents in DSPy agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In DSPy agents, Tool Loop Detection identifies infinite or excessive cycles where agents repeatedly invoke the same tools without progressing toward task completion, often due to optimized prompts overfitting to training examples that encourage redundant tool calls. This manifests uniquely in DSPy's composable module architecture, where ChainOfThought or ReAct modules can enter loops during `forward()` execution as the LM generates repetitive tool-calling signatures. Such loops waste tokens, exceed budget limits, and cause timeouts in production deployments.

howItManifests.symptom: Agent calls `search_tool` repeatedly with identical queries in a loop
howItManifests.impact: Token usage explodes; task never completes

howItManifests.symptom: Module outputs identical tool call signatures across iterations
howItManifests.impact: Infinite loop until max_steps exceeded; wrong final answer

howItManifests.symptom: Overfitted prompt always selects same tool regardless of context
howItManifests.impact: Stuck in single-tool feedback loop; ignores task requirements

howItManifests.symptom: Inner module loops trigger parent module recursion
howItManifests.impact: Stack overflow or timeout; assertion violations cascade

detection.name: Tool Call Signature Tracker
detection.description: Tracks repeated tool call patterns within DSPy module forward passes

detection.name: State Hash Monitor
detection.description: Detects loops by hashing agent state (context + tool history) across forward calls

detection.name: Call Stack Analyzer
detection.description: Inspects DSPy module call stack for recursive tool invocation patterns

fixes.name: Tool Call Blacklist
fixes.description: Prevents repeated tool calls by maintaining episodic memory of recent invocations

fixes.name: Diversity-Promoting Prompt
fixes.description: Injects explicit instructions to avoid tool repetition in DSPy signatures
fixes.code:
class AntiLoopSignature(dspy.Signature):
    '''IMPORTANT: Never call the same tool twice in a row. '''
    question = dspy.InputField()
    last_tool = dspy.InputField(default=None, desc='Previous tool name or null')
    recent_actions = dspy.InputField(default='[]')
    
    reasoning = dspy.OutputField(desc='''
    - Do NOT repeat the last_tool
    - Try different approach if stuck
    - If repeating tools, switch to final answer
    ''')
    
    action = dspy.OutputField(desc='''
    JSON: {"tool": "name", "params": {}} OR {"answer": "final"}
    ''' )

# Usage
agent = dspy.ChainOfThought(AntiLoopSignature)
pred = agent(question=question, last_tool=last_tool, recent_actions=recent_actions)

fixes.name: Max Steps Circuit Breaker
fixes.description: Hard limit on tool calls with graceful degradation in DSPy module composition

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Tool Loop Detection in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex provides production-grade automation for DSPy tool loop detection by wrapping module `forward()` calls with `vex.verify()`, automatically capturing execution traces, tool call histories, and state transitions. It employs statistical anomaly detection on tool invocation patterns and state hash sequences, flagging loops before they consume excessive tokens or timeout. 

Remediation happens inline: Vex injects diversity constraints into DSPy signatures, enforces tool call budgets, and applies gradient-free prompt optimization to break cyclic patterns—all without retraining. The platform surfaces root causes through DSPy-specific visualizations of module composition graphs and LM prediction distributions.

Integration requires only `pip install dspy vex-sdk` and a single decorator, enabling zero-config monitoring across DSPy programs in development, staging, and production environments.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK now and eliminate tool loops in your DSPy agents with one line of code.
